INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT

THIS INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT (this "Agreement") is entered into as of this 
25th day of April, 2023 (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

[LOGO]
ABC COMPANY, a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main St., New York, NY 10001 (hereinafter referred to as the "Company"); and

[LOGO]
JOHN DOE, an individual residing at 456 Elm St., Anytown, USA (hereinafter referred to as the "Contractor").

WHEREAS, the Company wishes to engage the services of the Contractor on a freelance basis for the development of certain software applications; and

WHEREAS, the Contractor is willing to provide such services to the Company subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. SCOPE OF WORK. The Contractor agrees to perform the services described in Exhibit A attached hereto (the "Services"). The Services are intended to be performed on a freelance basis and may include, without limitation, software development, testing and debugging.

2. COMPENSATION. The Company shall pay the Contractor for the Services at the rate of $100 per hour. Payments will be made within thirty (30) days of receipt by the Company of an invoice from the Contractor.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. All intellectual property rights in and to the Services, including, without limitation, software code, documentation, and any other materials created or delivered by the Contractor pursuant to this Agreement, shall vest in the Company upon delivery.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY. The Contractor agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all information relating to the Company's business, including, without limitation, trade secrets, customer lists, and financial information.

5. INDEMNIFICATION. The Contractor agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Company, its officers, directors, employees, agents and affiliates from any and all claims, damages, losses and expenses (including, without limitation, reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or in connection with the Services.

6. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other in the event of a material breach of this Agreement by the other party.

7. GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION. Any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

9. NOTICES. All notices and communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered personally, by facsimile transmission or by certified mail.

10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings and agreements between the parties hereto.

11. AMENDMENTS. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless made in writing signed by both parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

[Signature]

CONTRACTOR: _____________________________

COMPANY: _______________________________

By:

_____________________________

Its: _______________________________

INDEPENENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT THIS INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this           day of March, 2023 (the "Effective Date"), by and between XYZ CORPORATION (the "Client") a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with its principal place of business located at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA, and JOHN DOE (the "Independent Contractor") an individual residing at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, USA. WHEREAS, the Client desires to engage the services of the Independent Contractor as a freelancer for certain projects, and WHEREAS, the Independent Contractor is willing to provide such services on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows: 1. SCOPE OF WORK The Client shall engage the Independent Contractor to perform certain tasks and projects (the "Work") as more particularly described in Exhibit A attached hereto. The Work shall be performed by the Independent Contractor at the Client's offices located at 789 Oak Street, Anytown, USA. 2. TERM AND TERMINATION This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of one (1) year from the date hereof, unless terminated earlier in accordance with Section 4 below. Upon termination of this Agreement, the Client shall have no further obligation to pay the Independent Contractor any compensation or fees. 3. COMPENSATION The Client shall pay the Independent Contractor a fee of $50.00 per hour for all Work performed under this Agreement. The Client shall also reimburse the Independent Contractor for all reasonable expenses incurred in connection with the performance of the Work, including but not limited to travel expenses, meals, and lodging. 4. TERMINATION This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party in the event of a material breach of this Agreement by the other party. Upon termination, the Client shall pay the Independent Contractor all compensation due and owing as of the date of termination. 5. CONFIDENTIALITY The Independent Contractor agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all confidential information disclosed to him by the Client during the term of this Agreement. 6. INDEMNIFICATION The Independent Contractor agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Client against any claims, damages or expenses arising out of or in connection with the performance of the Work. 7. GOVERNING LAW This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware. Any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

THIS SOFTWARE AS A SERVICE AGREEMENT (THE "AGREEMENT") IS ENTERED INTO BY AND BETWEEN [CLIENT NAME], WITH ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS AT [CLIENT ADDRESS] (HEREINAFTER REFERRED TO AS THE "CLIENT"), AND [COMPANY NAME], A DELAWARE CORPORATION WITH ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS AT [COMPANY ADDRESS] (HEREINAFTER REFERRED TO AS THE "COMPANY") FOR THE USE OF COMPANY'S SOFTWARE AS A SERVICE PRODUCT, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION THE COMPANY'S [PRODUCT NAME] PRODUCT (THE "SERVICE").

1. DEFINITIONS.

For the purposes of this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

(a) Â "Confidential Information" means all confidential information disclosed by one Party to the other in writing or orally during the term of this Agreement, including without limitation trade secrets, know-how, business methods, research and development, software, technical data, designs, drawings, specifications, plans, customer lists, marketing strategies, and any other proprietary information.

(b) Â "Intellectual Property Rights" means all patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, know-how, and any other intellectual property rights of whatever nature whether registered or unregistered throughout the world including without limitation all applications for registration and all renewals and extensions thereof.

(c) Â "Service" means the Company's software as a service product, including without limitation the Company's [PRODUCT NAME] product.

(d) Â "Term" means the period commencing on the Effective Date and continuing until terminated in accordance with this Agreement.

(e) Â "Termination Fee" means a fee of $[AMOUNT] per month for each month remaining in the Term after termination, payable by Client to Company upon demand.

(f) Â "Warranties" mean all warranties, representations and undertakings made or implied by law or otherwise, including without limitation any express or implied warranty of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

MERGER AGREEMENT AND PLAN OF MERGER THIS M
erger Agreement and Plan of Merger (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 23rd day of February, 2023, by and between XYZ INCORPORATED, a Delaware corporation ("Buyer"), and ABC CORPORATION, a Delaware corporation ("Target").

WHEREAS, Buyer has determined to acquire the business and assets of Target; and

WHEREAS, the Board of Directors of each of Buyer and Target have approved this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. DEFINITIONS.

For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

"Business Day" means a day other than a Saturday, Sunday or any day on which banks in New York City are authorized by law to close;

"Closing Date" means the date on which the transactions contemplated hereby are consummated; and

"Effective Date" means the date on which this Agreement becomes effective.

2. ACQUISITION.

On the Closing Date, Buyer shall acquire all of the outstanding shares of common stock of Target (the "Target Shares") for a purchase price of $10,000,000.

3. TERMINATION.

This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other in the event that the Closing Date does not occur on or before March 31, 2023.

4. GOVERNING LAW.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

5. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, whether written or oral.

THIS VENDOR SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 23rd day of February, 2023 (the "Effective Date") by and between XYZ Corporation ("Vendor") with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA 12345 and ABC Company ("Customer") with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, USA 67890.

WHEREAS, the Vendor has agreed to provide certain services (the "Services") to the Customer; and

WHEREAS, the parties wish to establish the terms and conditions under which the Services shall be provided.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

 Article I: DEFINITIONS

1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

* "Service Level" means a measurable standard for the performance of the Services,

* "Service Credit" means a credit given to the Customer by the Vendor as compensation for a failure to meet a Service Level.

 Article II: SERVICES

2.1 The Vendor shall provide the Services with reasonable care and skill, in accordance with industry standards.

2.2 The Vendor shall use its best efforts to ensure that the Services are provided in a timely manner.

2.3 The Customer acknowledges that the Vendor may, at any time without notice to the Customer, modify or discontinue the Services, or any portion thereof.

 Article III: LIABILITY

3.1 IN NO EVENT SHALL THE VENDOR BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO LOST PROFITS, BUSINESS INTERRUPTIONS, OR LOSS OF DATA.

3.2 The Vendor's liability for any damages arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed $100,000.

 Article IV: TERMINATION

4.1 This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other in the event that the other party fails to comply with any term or condition of this Agreement.

4.2 Upon termination of this Agreement, the Customer shall immediately cease using the Services and return all copies of the Vendor's proprietary materials in its possession.

THIS MERGER AND ACQUISITION LETTER OF INTENT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 23rd day of February, Two Thousand Twenty-One (2021), by and between

ACME INCORPORATED, a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA 12345 (the "Buyer"); and

XYZ ENTERPRISES, LLC, a limited liability company organized under the laws of the State of New York with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, NY 67890 (the "Seller").

WHEREAS, the Buyer is seeking to acquire all of the issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of the Seller (the "Shares"); and

WHEREAS, the Seller is willing to sell the Shares to the Buyer on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Buyer" means Acme Incorporated, a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA 12345.

1.2 "Seller" means XYZ Enterprises, LLC, a limited liability company organized under the laws of the State of New York with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, NY 67890.

ARTICLE II: PURCHASE AND SALE

2.1 The Buyer agrees to purchase from the Seller all of the issued and outstanding Shares for a total consideration of $10,000,000 (the "Purchase Price").

2.2 The Purchase Price shall be paid by the Buyer in cash at closing.

ARTICLE III: CLOSING

3.1 Closing shall take place on or before March 31, 2021 (the "Closing Date") at a location to be determined by the Buyer.

3.2 At Closing, the Seller shall deliver to the Buyer all of the Shares and the Buyer shall pay the Purchase Price in accordance with Article II.

ARTICLE IV: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES

4.1 The Seller represents and warrants that:

(a) the Sellers have good title to the Shares;

(b) the Shares are free from any liens or encumbrances;

(c) the Seller has the right, power and authority to sell the Shares; and

d) all of the representations and warranties contained in this Article IV are true and accurate.

4.2 The Buyer represents and warrants that:

(a) the Buyer has the right, power and authority to purchase the Shares;

(b) the Buyer has sufficient funds to pay the Purchase Price; and

c) all of the representations and warranties contained in this Article IV are true and accurate.

ARTICLE V: INDEMNIFICATION

5.1 The Seller agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Buyer against any and all claims, demands, losses, damages or expenses arising out of or related to any breach by the Seller of its representations and warranties contained in this Agreement.

5.2 The Buyer agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Seller against any and all claims, demands, losses, damages or expenses arising out of or related to any breach by the Buyer of its representations and warranties contained in this Agreement.

ARTICLE VI: TERMINATION

6.1 This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party in the event that:

(a) the Closing Date shall not have occurred on or before March 31, 2021;

(b) any condition to closing set forth in this Agreement is not satisfied; or

c) any representation or warranty contained in this Agreement is breached.

6.2 Upon termination of this Agreement, all obligations and liabilities of the parties hereto shall cease and terminate.

ARTICLE VII: GOVERNING LAW

7.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.

7.2 Any dispute arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

BUYER:

By: _____________________________

Name: ___________________________

Title: ____________________________

SELLER:

By: _____________________________

Name: ___________________________

Title: ____________________________

THIS COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 20th day of March, 2023 (the "Effective Date") by and between

PARTY A: ABC Corporation (hereinafter referred to as "Landlord")
Address:
123 Main St.
Anytown, USA 12345

PARTY B: DEF Enterprises (hereinafter referred to as "Tenant")
Address:
456 Elm St.
Othertown, USA 54321

WHEREAS, Landlord is the owner of the premises located at 789 Oak St., Anytown, USA (the "Premises"); and

WHEREAS, Tenant desires to lease the Premises for a term of Three (3) years from the Effective Date; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. TERM.
The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of three (3) years thereafter, subject to the provisions of Section 15 below.

2. RENT.
Rent shall be paid by Tenant to Landlord in advance on or before the first day of each month during the term of this Agreement, in the amount of $5,000 per month, payable to Landlord at:

Landlord's Address: 123 Main St., Anytown, USA 12345

3. USE OF PREMISES.
Tenant shall use the Premises solely for office purposes and shall not sublease or assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of Landlord.

4. MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS.
Tenant shall maintain the Premises in good repair and condition, reasonable wear and tear excepted, and shall make all necessary repairs to keep the Premises in good working order.

5. UTILITIES.
Landlord shall be responsible for paying all utility bills associated with the Premises, including but not limited to electricity, gas, water, and sewer.

6. INSURANCE.
Tenant shall obtain and maintain at its own expense a policy of liability insurance naming Landlord as an additional insured.

7. DEFAULT AND REMEDY.
If Tenant fails to comply with any provision of this Agreement, Landlord may terminate this Agreement and take possession of the Premises, or seek damages for breach.

8. NOTICES.
All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and served upon the other party by personal delivery or certified mail.

9. GOVERNING LAW.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Anytown, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

10. AMENDMENTS.
No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be binding upon either party unless made in writing and signed by both parties.

11. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between the parties.

12. DISPUTE RESOLUTION.
Any disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

13. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY.
In no event shall Landlord's liability for any damages, claims, or demands exceed $100,000.00.

14. TERMINATION.
This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party, provided that such termination does not result in a material breach of this Agreement.

15. DEFAULT.
If Tenant fails to pay rent when due, or if Tenant breaches any provision of this Agreement, Landlord may terminate this Agreement and take possession of the Premises.

16. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Anytown, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

17. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between the parties.

18. DISPUTE RESOLUTION.
Any disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

19. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY.
In no event shall Landlord's liability for any damages, claims, or demands exceed $100,000.00.

20. TERMINATION.
This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party, provided that such termination does not result in a material breach of this Agreement.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

{"parties": ["ABC Corporation", "DEF Enterprises"], "effective_date": "20th day of March, 2023", "liability_cap": "$100,000.00", "termination_conditions": "Written notice to the other party; material breach of this Agreement"}

EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT

THIS EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT (this "Agreement") is made and entered into as of the date first below written (the "Effective Date") by and between:

EMPLOYEE NAME: John Doe
TITLE: Chief Executive Officer
ADDRESS: 123 Main St, Anytown, USA 12345
PHONE: 555-555-5555
EMAIL: johndoe@email.com

COMPANY NAME: ABC Corporation
ADDRESS: 456 Elm St, Othertown, USA 67890

WHEREAS, the Company is desirous of employing Employee as its Chief Executive Officer; and
WHEREAS, Employee is willing to accept such employment on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement;
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. TERM AND TERMINATION.
    a. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of two (2) years from the date first above written.
    b. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, Employee shall be entitled to receive all accrued but unpaid vacation time in accordance with the Company's policies.

2. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS.
    a. The Company agrees to pay Employee an annual salary of $200,000, plus an additional bonus of twenty percent (20%) of his base salary, payable quarterly.
    b. Employee shall also be entitled to participate in the Company's health insurance plan and 401(k) plan on the same terms as other employees.

3. CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.
    a. During the term of this Agreement, Employee agrees not to disclose any confidential information of the Company without prior written authorization.
    b. All inventions, patents, and other intellectual property developed by Employee during his employment with the Company shall be owned exclusively by the Company.

4. TERMINATION FOR CAUSE.
    a. In the event of termination for cause, the Company may terminate this Agreement immediately upon providing written notice to Employee.
    b. Termination for cause shall include, but not be limited to, gross misconduct, theft, or other conduct deemed detrimental to the business of the Company.

5. GOVERNING LAW.
    a. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Anytown.
    b. The parties agree to submit any disputes arising out of this Agreement to binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

EMPLOYEE: John Doe
COMPANY: ABC Corporation

By:

John Doe
Chief Executive Officer
ABC Corporation

SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT
THIS SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT (THIS "AGREEMENT") IS ENTERED INTO ON THE DATE FIRST BELOW WRITTEN (THE "EFFECTIVE DATE") BY AND BETWEEN [VENDOR], A DELAWARE CORPORATION WITH ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS AT 123 MAIN STREET, SUITE 400, NEW YORK, NY 10001 ("VENDOR"); AND [CUSTOMER], A COMPANY WITH ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS AT [ADDRESS] ("CUSTOMER")
ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS
FOR THE PURPOSES OF THIS AGREEMENT, THE FOLLOWING TERMS SHALL HAVE THE MEANINGS SET FORTH BELOW:
1. "SERVICES" MEANS VENDOR'S OBLIGATIONS TO PROVIDE AND CUSTOMER'S RIGHT TO RECEIVE CERTAIN SERVICES AS MORE FULLY DESCRIBED IN SCHEDULE 1 HERETO.
2. "SERVICE LEVELS" MEANS THE SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE METRICS AND TARGET VALUES SET FORTH IN SECTION 3 BELOW.
ARTICLE II: SERVICE OBLIGATIONS
VENDOR SHALL PROVIDE THE SERVICES TO CUSTOMER WITHIN THE TIME FRAMES AND ACCORDING TO THE PROCESS AND PROCEDURES DESCRIBED IN SCHEDULE 1 HERETO. VENDOR SHALL USE REASONABLE CARE AND SKILL IN PROVIDING THE SERVICES.
ARTICLE III: SERVICE LEVELS
VENDOR SHALL ENSURE THAT ITS PERFORMANCE OF THE SERVICES MEETS OR EXCEEDS THE FOLLOWING SERVICE LEVELS:
A. RESPONSE TIME: VENDOR SHALL RESPOND TO CUSTOMER'S REQUEST FOR ASSISTANCE WITHIN 2 HOURS OF RECEIPT.
B. INCIDENT RESOLUTION: VENDOR SHALL RESOLVE CUSTOMER'S REPORTED INCIDENTS WITHIN 4 HOURS OR LESS, PROVIDED THAT THE INCIDENT IS REASONABLY CAUSED BY VENDOR.
ARTICLE IV: LIABILITY
VENDOR'S LIABILITY FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED $1,000,000.00 (THE "LIABILITY CAP").
ARTICLE V: TERMINATION

RECITALS WHEREAS, BUYER IS INTERESTED IN ACQUIRING THE SHARES OF SELLER AND HAS DETERMINED THAT SUCH ACQUISITION WOULD BE BENEFICIAL TO ITS BUSINESS; AND WHEREAS, SELLER IS WILLING TO CONSIDER A SALE OF ITS SHARES PURSUANT TO THIS LOI. NOW THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION OF THE MUTUAL COVENANTS AND PROMISES CONTAINED HEREIN, THE PARTIES AGREE AS FOLLOWS: 1. DEFINITIONS The terms defined in this Article I shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this Article and in the context where they are used throughout this LOI. For purposes of this LOI, "Shares" means all issued and outstanding shares of common stock of Seller. "Company" means Seller and its subsidiaries. 2. PURCHASE PRICE AND TERMS The purchase price for the Shares (the "Purchase Price") shall be $10 million, subject to adjustment as provided in Article III below. BUYER SHALL PAY THE PURCHASE PRICE IN CASH AT THE CLOSING. 3. DUE DILIGENCE BUYER SHALL HAVE THE RIGHT TO CONDUCT DUE DILIGENCE WITH RESPECT TO SELLER AND ITS SUBSIDIARIES FOR A PERIOD OF FORTY-FIVE (45) DAYS FOLLOWING THE DATE OF THIS LOI. 4. CONDITIONS PRECEDENT TO CLOSING The closing of the acquisition contemplated hereby (the "Closing") shall be subject to the satisfaction or waiver by BUYER and SELLER of the following conditions: A. BUYER SHALL HAVE CONDUCTED ITS DUE DILIGENCE IN ACCORDANCE WITH ARTICLE III ABOVE; B. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF SELLER SHALL HAVE APPROVED THE SALE OF SHARES PURSUANT TO THIS LOI; C. ALL REQUISITE REGISTRATIONS AND FILINGS UNDER THE SECURITIES ACT OF 1933, AS AMENDED, SHALL HAVE BEEN MADE OR WAIVED BY BUYER AND SELLER; D. ALL OTHER REQUISITE CORPORATE ACTION SHALL HAVE BEEN TAKEN BY BUYER AND SELLER; E. THERE SHALL BE NO LITIGATION PENDING AGAINST SELLER THAT WOULD INTERFERE WITH THE SALE OF SHARES PURSUANT TO THIS LOI; F. BUYER SHALL HAVE RECEIVED A COPY OF ALL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO SELLER'S SUBSIDIARIES, INCLUDING FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND TAX RETURNS FOR THE LAST TWO YEARS. 5. TERMINATION This LOI may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other if such termination is not in breach of any obligation hereunder. The termination shall take effect on the date specified in the written notice and shall not affect any liability that had accrued prior thereto. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, THE PARTIES HAVE EXECUTED THIS LOI AS OF THE DATE FIRST ABOVE WRITTEN.

This Merger and Acquisition Letter of Intent (this "Agreement") is entered into on [DATE] (the "Effective Date") by and between:

[PARTY A]:

ABC Corporation, a Delaware corporation (the "Company"), with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA 12345 (the "Principal Place of Business"); and

[PARTY B]:

XYZ Holdings, Inc., a Delaware corporation (the "Purchaser"), with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, USA 67890 (the "Principal Place of Business").

WHEREAS, the Company and the Purchaser are desirous of effecting a merger or acquisition transaction (the "Transaction") whereby the Purchaser shall acquire all of the outstanding shares of common stock of the Company (the "Shares"); and

WHEREAS, the parties desire to set forth their understanding regarding the terms and conditions of the Transaction.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. The Purchaser shall have the right to acquire all of the Shares for a purchase price (the "Purchase Price") equal to $10,000,000 (Ten Million Dollars), subject to adjustment as provided below.

2. The Purchase Price shall be paid in cash upon closing of the Transaction.

3. The Company shall prepare and deliver to the Purchaser a balance sheet as of the date hereof (the "Balance Sheet") and a detailed list of all assets and liabilities of the Company (the "Asset List").

4. The Purchaser may, at its option, reduce the Purchase Price by an amount equal to any losses incurred by the Company in connection with any business or operation that is discontinued prior to the closing date.

5. The Transaction shall be consummated on the earlier of (i) the 120th day after the date hereof (the "Closing Date") or (ii) the date on which all conditions to closing have been satisfied or waived, whichever shall first occur.

6. In the event that the Purchaser fails to consummate the Transaction within the time period specified in Section 5 above, the Company may terminate this Agreement by giving written notice to the Purchaser.

7. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other if the other party shall have failed to comply with any of the terms and conditions hereof, or if a court of competent jurisdiction shall have enjoined, restrained or prohibited the consummation of the Transaction.

8. The Company and the Purchaser agree to use their respective best efforts to obtain all necessary approvals and consents from regulatory bodies and other third parties in connection with the Transaction.

9. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings and agreements between the parties.

10. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by a written instrument signed by both parties.

11. The headings contained in this Agreement are for convenience only and shall not affect the construction or interpretation of any provision hereof.

12. This Agreement shall be governed by, construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

13. Any action brought pursuant to this Agreement shall be instituted in the courts of the State of Delaware.

14. The parties hereto acknowledge that they have read and understand all of the provisions contained herein, including the termination conditions set forth in Section 7 above.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

MERGER AND ACQUISITION LETTER OF INTENT

This Merger and Acquisition Letter of Intent (the "Letter of Intent") is entered into on this 23rd day of February, 2023 (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

ACME INCORPORATED, a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA 12345 (the "Buyer"); and

XYZ ENTERPRISES, INC., a California corporation with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, CA 98765 (the "Seller").

WHEREAS, the Buyer is desirous of acquiring all of the issued and outstanding shares of common stock of the Seller (the "Shares"); and

WHEREAS, the Seller is willing to sell the Shares to the Buyer on the terms and conditions set forth in this Letter of Intent.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. PURCHASE PRICE.

The purchase price for the acquisition of the Shares (the "Purchase Price") shall be $10,000,000 (ten million dollars), subject to adjustment as set forth in Section 5 hereof.

2. PAYMENT TERMS.

The Purchase Price shall be paid by the Buyer in cash upon closing of the transaction, which is anticipated to occur within twelve months from the Effective Date.

3. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES.

The Seller represents and warrants that:

(a) it has the right, power and authority to sell the Shares; and

(b) the Shares are free from any liens or encumbrances.

4. CLOSING CONDITIONS.

The closing of the transaction shall be subject to the satisfaction of the following conditions precedent: (i) the Buyer obtaining satisfactory title insurance covering the Shares; and (ii) the Seller delivering a certificate of ownership for the Shares.

5. ADJUSTMENTS TO PURCHASE PRICE.

In the event that the Seller's financial statements as of the date of closing show net income in excess of $1,000,000, the Purchase Price shall be adjusted by an amount equal to 50% of such excess.

6. TERMINATION.

This Letter of Intent may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other within ten days after the occurrence of any of the following events: (i) a material breach of this Letter of Intent by the other party; or (ii) a change in control of the Seller.

7. GOVERNING LAW.

This Letter of Intent shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Letter of Intent as of the date first above written.

This Servie Level Agreement ('SLA') is entered into on [Not Specified] (the 'Effective Date') by and between VendorX, a company registered in Delaware, USA, with principal place of business at 123 Main St, Anytown, USA 12345 (the 'Vendor'), and CustomerY, a company registered in California, USA, with principal place of business at 456 Elm St, Othertown, USA 67890 (the 'Customer').

1. DEFINITIONS.
The following definitions shall apply throughout this SLA: 

'Agreed Service Level' means the percentage of time that the Vendor's services are available and functioning as specified in the applicable Service Description; 

'Service Description' means the written description of the services to be provided by the Vendor, as attached hereto as Schedule A;

'Uptime' means the period during which the Vendor's services are available for use without interruption.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF VENDOR.
The Vendor shall: 

(a) provide the Customer with access to the services described in the Service Description; 

(b) ensure that all services provided hereunder meet or exceed the standards specified in the Service Description;

(c) maintain a minimum uptime of 99.9% during any calendar month.

3. REMEDIES FOR BREACH.
The Vendor shall be liable for breach of this SLA as follows: 

(a) in the event that the Vendor fails to meet or exceed the standards specified in the Service Description, the Customer may terminate this SLA upon written notice to the Vendor;

(b) in the event that the Vendor's uptime falls below 99.9% during any calendar month, the Vendor shall pay to the Customer a service credit of $1,000 per hour of downtime.

4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY.
The Vendor's liability for breach of this SLA shall be limited as follows: 

(a) in no event shall the Vendor be liable for consequential damages, including but not limited to loss of profits or business interruption;

(b) in no event shall the Vendor be liable for more than $100,000 per year.

5. TERMINATION.
This SLA may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other.

6. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.
This SLA shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California,

7. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.
This SLA constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings.

This Letter of Intent ('LOI') is entered into as of the date first below written ('Effective Date') by and between

[SPACER] ABC INVESTMENTS, LLC (the 'Buyer'), a Delaware limited liability company with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Suite 456, New York, NY 10001,

and [SPACER] XYZ HOLDINGS CORP. (the 'Seller'), a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 789 Wall Street, Suite 101, New York, NY 10007.

WHEREAS, the Buyer desires to acquire and the Seller is willing to sell all of the issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of XYZ Holdings Corp. ('Target');

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. DEFINITIONS.
A. The term 'Merger' shall mean the merger of Target with and into Buyer pursuant to Section 253 of the Delaware General Corporation Law.
B. The term 'Purchase Price' shall mean $10,000,000 (ten million dollars) payable by Buyer upon Closing.
C. The term 'Closing' shall mean the date on which the Merger is consummated.

2. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES.
A. Seller represents and warrants that:
1. Target has been duly organized and is validly existing under the laws of Delaware;
2. Target has all necessary power and authority to enter into this LOI and to perform its obligations hereunder;
3. The execution, delivery and performance of this LOI by Seller does not and will not violate any agreement or instrument binding upon Seller.

B. Buyer represents and warrants that:
1. Buyer has been duly organized and is validly existing under the laws of Delaware;
2. Buyer has all necessary power and authority to enter into this LOI and to perform its obligations hereunder;
3. The execution, delivery and performance of this LOI by Buyer does not and will not violate any agreement or instrument binding upon Buyer.

3. TERMINATION.
A. This LOI may be terminated at the election of either party by written notice to the other party in accordance with Section 5 hereof.
B. Upon termination of this LOI, all obligations of the parties hereunder shall cease and terminate, except as otherwise provided herein.

4. CLOSING.
A. Closing shall occur on the date specified in Section 3.A. above, unless otherwise agreed to by the parties in writing.
B. At Closing, Buyer shall pay to Seller an amount equal to the Purchase Price minus any amounts previously paid under this LOI.
C. Upon payment of the Purchase Price, all issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of Target shall be delivered to Buyer.

5. GOVERNING LAW.
A. This LOI shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.
B. Any dispute arising out of or related to this LOI shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

6. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.
A. This LOI constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings and agreements between them.
B. This LOI may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.

EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT

THIS EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT ("CONTRACT") IS ENTERED INTO ON THIS 20th DAY OF FEBRUARY, TWO THOUSAND AND TWENTY-NINE, BY AND BETWEEN:

[COMPANY NAME], a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at [ADDRESS] (the "COMPANY"); and

[EXECUTIVE'S NAME], an individual residing at [ADDRESS] (the "EXECUTIVE").

RECITALS

WHEREAS, the COMPANY is desirous of employing the EXECUTIVE as its Chief Executive Officer; and

WHEREAS, the EXECUTIVE is willing to accept such employment on the terms and conditions set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: TERM AND TERMINATION

1. The term of this CONTRACT shall commence on the date hereof (the "EFFECTIVE DATE") and continue for a period of five (5) years from such date, subject to earlier termination as provided in Article VII.

2. Upon expiration or termination of this CONTRACT, the EXECUTIVE shall cease to be employed by the COMPANY, and all rights and privileges granted hereunder shall terminate.

ARTICLE II: EMPLOYMENT STATUS AND RESPONSIBILITIES

1. The EXECUTIVE shall serve as Chief Executive Officer of the COMPANY, reporting directly to the BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

2. The EXECUTIVE shall perform such duties and have such responsibilities as may be assigned by the BOARD OF DIRECTORS from time to time.

ARTICLE III: COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

1. During the term of this CONTRACT, the COMPANY shall pay the EXECUTIVE an annual base salary of $500,000, payable in accordance with the COMPANY'S PAYROLL PRACTICES.

2. The EXECUTIVE shall also be entitled to participate in the COMPANY'S EMPLOYEE BENEFIT PLANS, including but not limited to health insurance, retirement plan, and stock option plan, on the same terms and conditions as other executives of the COMPANY.

ARTICLE IV: CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

1. The EXECUTIVE agrees that during the term of this CONTRACT and for a period of one (1) year thereafter, he shall not disclose any confidential information relating to the COMPANY or its business.

2. The EXECUTIVE acknowledges that all inventions, improvements, and trade secrets developed by him while employed by the COMPANY are the sole property of the COMPANY.

ARTICLE V: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

1. This CONTRACT shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.

2. Any disputes arising out of or relating to this CONTRACT shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

ARTICLE VI: NOTICES

1. All notices, demands, and communications required or permitted to be given under this CONTRACT shall be in writing and delivered personally or sent by certified mail, return receipt requested.

2. Any notice or communication so sent shall be deemed to have been given on the date of delivery or mailing, as the case may be.

ARTICLE VII: TERMINATION FOR CAUSE

1. The COMPANY may terminate this CONTRACT immediately upon written notice to the EXECUTIVE in the event that the EXECUTIVE is convicted of a felony or is adjudged mentally incompetent.

2. The COMPANY may also terminate this CONTRACT if the EXECUTIVE commits any material breach of his duties or obligations hereunder, which breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice thereof by the COMPANY.

ARTICLE VIII: ENTIRE AGREEMENT

1. This CONTRACT constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between them.

2. There are no oral or written agreements or understandings that are not fully expressed in this CONTRACT.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this CONTRACT as of the date first above written.

[COMPANY NAME]
By:
[EXECUTIVE'S NAME]

ACME INCORPORATED, a Delaware corporation (the "Buyer"), with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA 12345;
and

XYZ ENTERPRISES, LLC, a limited liability company organized under the laws of the State of New York (the "Seller"), with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, NY 67890.

WHEREAS, the Buyer is desirous of acquiring all of the issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of the Seller (the "Shares"); and

WHEREAS, the Seller is willing to sell the Shares to the Buyer on the terms and conditions set forth in this LOI;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. Purchase Price. The Buyer shall pay to the Seller a purchase price (the "Purchase Price") equal to Two Hundred Million Dollars ($200,000,000).
2. Liability Cap. In no event shall either party be liable for any damages or losses in excess of Fifty Million Dollars ($50,000,000) arising out of or related to this LOI or the transactions contemplated hereby.
3. Termination Conditions. This LOI may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other if:
a. The other party fails to perform any material obligation under this LOI; or
b. Any governmental authority issues a ruling or regulation that makes it illegal for the Buyer to consummate the transactions contemplated hereby.
4. Confidentiality. Each party agrees to keep confidential all information disclosed by the other party in connection with this LOI, unless disclosure is required by law or regulatory requirement.
5. Governing Law. This LOI shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this LOI as of the date first above written.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT
This Commercial Lease Agreement (the ".Agreement") is entered into on this 2nd day of March, 2023 (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

LANDLORD: XYZ CORPORATION,
Address: 123 Main St, Anytown, USA 12345

and

TENANT: ABC INC.,
Address: 456 Elm St, Othertown, USA 67890

WHEREAS, the Landlord desires to lease certain commercial property (the "Premises") located at [insert address] in Anytown, USA; and

WHEREAS, the Tenant is willing to rent said Premises on the terms and conditions contained herein.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: TERM AND RENT
1. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of three (3) years from said date, unless terminated earlier in accordance with Article VII.
2. The annual rent for the Premises shall be $150,000.00 per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $12,500.00 each, on or before the 1st day of each month.

ARTICLE II: USE OF PREMISES
The Tenant shall use the Premises only for general office purposes and shall not conduct any hazardous or noxious activities thereon.

ARTICLE III: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS
1. The Landlord shall be responsible for maintaining the structural integrity of the Premises, including but not limited to, the roof, walls, and foundation.
2. The Tenant shall be responsible for all maintenance and repairs to the interior of the Premises, including but not limited to, the electrical, plumbing, and HVAC systems.

ARTICLE IV: UTILITIES AND SERVICES
1. The Landlord shall provide the Tenant with access to the following utilities: electricity, water, gas, and trash removal.
2. The Tenant shall be responsible for all telephone, internet, and other services utilized in connection with the Premises.

ARTICLE V: INSURANCE
The Tenant shall maintain liability insurance on the Premises in an amount not less than $1,000,000.00 per occurrence.

ARTICLE VI: DEFAULT AND REMEDY
1. If the Tenant fails to pay rent when due, or breaches any other provision of this Agreement, the Landlord may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the Tenant.
2. In the event of termination, the Tenant shall vacate the Premises immediately and surrender possession thereof to the Landlord.

ARTICLE VII: TERMINATION
This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other in accordance with Article VI.

ARTICLE VIII: GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Anytown. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

Title: ___________________________________

TENANT:
Signature: _______________________________

Title: ___________________________________

This Merger Agreement and Plan of Merger (the "Agreement") is entered into as of [DATE] by and between [PARTY A], a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at [ADDRESS] (the "Company"), and [PARTY B], a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at [ADDRESS] (the "Purchaser").

WHEREAS, the Company is desirous of effecting a merger with the Purchaser pursuant to Section 253 of the General Corporation Law of the State of Delaware; and

WHEREAS, the Purchaser has agreed to acquire all of the outstanding capital stock of the Company in exchange for the delivery of shares of its common stock (the "Consideration"); and

WHEREAS, the parties wish to enter into this Agreement to set forth the terms and conditions of the merger.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1.  The Company shall cause to be prepared and delivered to the Purchaser a certificate (the "Certificate") setting forth the capitalization of the Company as of the date hereof.

2.  Upon receipt of the Certificate, the Purchaser shall deliver to the Company shares of its common stock (the "Purchased Shares") in an amount equal to the number of shares specified in the Certificate.

3.  The Purchaser shall cause to be filed with the Secretary of State of Delaware a certificate of merger setting forth the terms and conditions of this Agreement, which certificate of merger shall recite that all of the outstanding capital stock of the Company has been acquired by the Purchaser in exchange for shares of its common stock.

4.  The Purchaser shall cause to be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission a registration statement under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Registration Statement"), which Registration Statement shall include information regarding the Company's capitalization as of the date hereof and the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

5.  The Purchaser shall cause to be issued to the shareholders of the Company shares of its common stock in exchange for their outstanding capital stock, subject to the provisions of Section 253 of the General Corporation Law of the State of Delaware.

6.  Upon completion of the merger, the Purchaser shall take all necessary actions to ensure that the Certificate is properly filed with the Secretary of State of Delaware and that the Registration Statement is properly filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

7.  The parties agree that this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between them regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between them.

8.  This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.

9.  This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without regard to its conflict of law principles.

10. In the event that any provision hereof is held invalid or unenforceable, such invalidity or unenforceability shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining provisions hereof.

11. The parties agree to execute and deliver all documents necessary to effectuate the merger and to perform their respective obligations under this Agreement.

12. The parties acknowledge that they have read and understand the terms and conditions of this Agreement and are executing it voluntarily without coercion or duress.

13. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, assigns, heirs, and personal representatives.

14. The parties agree that they have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have hereunto set their hands and seals the day and year first above written.

ACME CORPORATION, a Delaware corporation (the "Buyer"); and

XYZ INCORPORATED, a California corporation (the "Target").

WHEREAS, the Buyer is desirous of acquiring the entire issued and outstanding capital stock of the Target; and

WHEREAS, the Target is willing to consider such acquisition on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in this LOI;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS

For purposes of this LOI, the following definitions shall apply:

1. "Closing" means the closing date for the transaction contemplated hereby.

2. "Effective Date" means the date on which the Merger is consummated.

3. "Merger" means the merger of the Target into the Buyer in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth in Article III below.

4. "Target Stock" means all of the issued and outstanding capital stock of the Target.

ARTICLE II: TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE PROPOSED ACQUISITION

The Buyer proposes to acquire the entire issued and outstanding capital stock of the Target (the "Proposed Acquisition") for a purchase price (the "Purchase Price") of $10,000,000.00 (ten million dollars).

The Proposed Acquisition is subject to certain conditions set forth in Article III below.

ARTICLE III: CONDITIONS TO CLOSING

The closing date for the transaction contemplated hereby shall be on or before March 31st, 2022 (the "Closing Date"). The Buyer and the Target agree that the Closing may be delayed by up to thirty (30) days if the Buyer is unable to complete its due diligence review of the Target's business and financial affairs.

ARTICLE IV: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES

The Target represents and warrants to the Buyer that:

1. The Target has the power and authority to enter into this LOI and to perform all of the obligations hereunder.

2. The Target will not engage in any business or activity that would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on its business, operations, properties, assets or financial condition.

3. The Target has made no representations or warranties to any third party with respect to the Proposed Acquisition.

ARTICLE V: TERMINATION

Either party may terminate this LOI upon written notice to the other if the Closing does not occur by March 31st, 2022.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this LOI as of the date first above written.

ACME CORPORATION

By: ______________________

Title: ______________________

XYZ INCORPORATED

By: ______________________

Title: ______________________

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE 1: TERM AND TERMINATION

The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and continue for a period of twelve (12) months from the Effective Date. Upon expiration of said term, this Agreement may be renewed for additional one-year periods upon written notice by either party to the other at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then current term.

ARTICLE 2: SERVICES

The Client shall provide the Independent Contractor with all necessary information and materials required for the performance of the Services. The Independent Contractor shall be responsible for all expenses incurred in connection with the performance of the Services.

ARTICLE 3: COMPENSATION

The Client shall pay the Independent Contractor a fee of $50,000 per annum (the "Fees") for the provision of the Services. Payment of the Fees shall be made on or before the last day of each month following the end of the preceding quarter.

ARTICLE 4: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

The Client reserves all right, title and interest in and to any intellectual property developed by the Independent Contractor in connection with the performance of the Services. The Independent Contractor assigns to the Client all right, title and interest in and to such intellectual property.

The Independent Contractor shall also indemnify and hold harmless the Client for any loss or damage caused by the negligence or wilful misconduct of the Independent Contractor.

ARTICLE 7: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

The liability of the Client to the Independent Contractor under this Agreement shall not exceed $100,000 (the "Liability Cap"). The Client's liability to the Independent Contractor for any breach of this Agreement shall be limited to the amount of Fees paid by the Client to the Independent Contractor in the twelve-month period preceding such breach.

ARTICLE 8: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Anytown. Any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

ARTICLE 9: ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings and agreements between the parties. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.

CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE

THIS CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 1st day of March, 2023 (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

LANDLORD: JOHN DOE, a single person with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA (the "Landlord"); and

TENANT: ABC CORPORATION, a corporation duly organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York, having its principal place of business at 456 Broadway, New York, NY 10013 (the "Tenant").

WHEREAS, the Landlord is the owner of the premises located at 789 Elm Street, Anytown, USA (the "Premises"); and

WHEREAS, the Tenant desires to lease the Premises for commercial purposes; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. TERM: The term of this Agreement shall commence on April 1, 2023 (the "Commencement Date") and shall continue for a period of five (5) years from the Commencement Date (the "Initial Term"). Upon expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement may be renewed for additional periods of one (1) year each, provided that either party gives written notice to the other at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term.

2. RENT: The Tenant shall pay rent in the amount of $10,000 per month, payable on or before the 1st day of each month. Rent shall be paid to the Landlord at the address specified above.

3. SECURITY DEPOSIT: The Tenant shall deposit with the Landlord a security deposit in the amount of $20,000 within five (5) days after signing this Agreement. The security deposit shall be refundable upon termination of this Agreement, provided that all obligations of the Tenant under this Agreement have been fulfilled.

4. USE OF PREMISES: The Tenant agrees to use the Premises only for commercial purposes and not to sublease or assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the Landlord.

5. MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS: The Tenant shall be responsible for maintaining the Premises in good repair and condition, reasonable wear and tear excepted. The Tenant shall also be responsible for any damages caused by its negligence or willful misconduct.

6. INSURANCE: The Tenant shall maintain liability insurance with a minimum limit of $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate. The Tenant shall also name the Landlord as an additional insured on all policies.

7. DEFAULT: If the Tenant fails to pay rent or otherwise comply with any provision of this Agreement, the Landlord may terminate this Agreement and re-enter the Premises without notice.

8. GOVERNING LAW: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION: Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between them.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

LANDLORD: JOHN DOE

TENANT: ABC CORPORATION

NOW THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION OF THE MUTUAL COVENANTS AND PROMISES CONTAINED HEREIN,

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS.

1.1 "Service" means the software as a service (SaaS) solutions provided by PARTY A to PARTY B under this Agreement;

ARTICLE II: SERVICE LEVELS.
The parties agree that PARTY A shall provide the Service with a minimum uptime of 99.9% per annum, as measured over a calendar month (the "Uptime Guarantee"). ARTICLE III: FEES AND PAYMENT TERMS.

ARTICLE IV: CONFIDENTIALITY.
Each party agrees not to disclose to any third party (other than its auditors, lawyers and other professional advisers who need to know such information for the purpose of advising such party) any confidential information concerning the other party or its business which it may obtain as a result of this Agreement.

ARTICLE V: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.
The parties agree that all intellectual property rights in the Service shall vest in PARTY A. PARTY B agrees not to challenge the validity of any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right vested in PARTY A.

ARTICLE VI: TERMINATION.
This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon one month's written notice to the other. Upon termination, PARTY B shall pay all Fees due and payable up to the date of termination.

ARTICLE VII: GOVERNING LAW.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [STATE/COUNTRY]. The parties agree that any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of [STATE/COUNTRY].

EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT
THIS EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT (the "Agreement") is made and entered into on this 23rd day of February, 2023, by and between

RICHARD PATEL ("Executive")
Address: 1234 Oak Street, Apt. 5, New York, NY 10001

and

XYZ CORPORATION (the "Company")
Address: 5678 Maple Street, Suite 200, Chicago, IL 60611

ARTICLE I: TERM AND TERMINATION
1. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and shall continue for a period of three (3) years from the date of commencement, subject to earlier termination as provided in Article VII.
2. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Executive's employment with the Company shall automatically terminate, except that any accrued but unpaid vacation time and other benefits shall be paid out in accordance with the Company's usual practices.

ARTICLE II: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
1. The Executive shall perform such duties as may be assigned to him by the Board of Directors (the "Board") from time to time, including but not limited to serving as Chief Operating Officer and performing such other duties as are customary for an executive officer of the Company.
2. The Executive shall devote his full business time and best efforts to the performance of his duties hereunder and shall not engage in any other occupation or activity that is inconsistent with the terms of this Agreement.

ARTICLE III: COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS
1. The Company shall pay the Executive an annual salary of $250,000, paid bi-weekly in accordance with the Company's usual practices.
2. The Executive shall be eligible to participate in all benefit plans and programs made available to executive officers of the Company, including but not limited to health insurance, life insurance, and disability insurance.

ARTICLE IV: CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
1. The Executive acknowledges that he has access to confidential information of the Company, including trade secrets and other proprietary information.
2. The Executive agrees to keep all such information confidential and not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent of the Board.
3. The Executive acknowledges that all inventions, discoveries, and improvements made by him during his employment with the Company shall belong exclusively to the Company.

ARTICLE V: NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS
1. Any notice or communication required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered personally or sent by certified mail, return receipt requested.
2. The Executive's address for purposes of this Article shall be his address as set forth above, and the Company's address shall be its address as set forth above.

ARTICLE VI: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
1. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.
2. Any dispute arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

ARTICLE VII: TERMINATION FOR CAUSE
1. The Company may terminate the Executive's employment for cause upon written notice to him, specifying the grounds for termination, if any of the following events occur:
a) The Executive commits a material breach of this Agreement or any other agreement with the Company;
b) The Executive is convicted of a felony or misdemeanor involving moral turpitude;
c) The Executive engages in conduct that constitutes gross negligence or willful misconduct.

ARTICLE VIII: ENTIRE AGREEMENT
1. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between them.
2. No amendment to this Agreement shall be valid unless it is in writing and signed by both parties.

MERGER AGREEMENT AND PLAN OF MERGER

THIS LETTER OF INTENT (this "Letter of Intent"), dated as of December 13, 2022 (the "Effective Date"), is entered into by and between:

SMITHSONIAN ENTERPRISES LLC (the "Seller") with a principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA, and 

ACME INCORPORATED (the "Purchaser") with a principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, USA.

WHEREAS, the Seller is the owner of certain assets (the "Assets"), including all right, title, and interest in and to certain patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, know-how, software, databases, and other intangible and tangible properties (collectively, the "Intellectual Property");

WHEREAS, the Purchaser desires to acquire the Assets from the Seller for a purchase price of $10,000,000.00 (the "Purchase Price") upon the terms and conditions set forth herein; and

WHEREAS, the Seller is willing to sell the Assets to the Purchaser on the terms and conditions set forth herein.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS

1.1 Definitions.

For purposes of this Letter of Intent, the following definitions shall apply:

(a) "Affiliate" means any Person directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such Person;

(b) "Business Day" means a day other than a Saturday, Sunday or other day on which banks in New York City are authorized or required to be closed;

(c) "Closing" means the closing of the transactions contemplated hereby; and

(d) "Person" means any natural person, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, trust, unincorporated organization, association, or other entity.

ARTICLE II: ACQUISITION OF ASSETS

2.1 Acquisition. Upon the terms and conditions set forth herein, the Purchaser shall acquire from the Seller all of the right, title, and interest in and to the Assets.

2.2 Purchase Price. The purchase price for the Assets shall be $10,000,000.00 (the "Purchase Price") payable at Closing.

ARTICLE III: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES

3.1 Representations and Warranties of Seller.

The Seller represents and warrants to the Purchaser that:

(a) it has the right, power, and authority to enter into this Letter of Intent and to sell the Assets to the Purchaser;

(b) the Assets are free from any liens, encumbrances, or other restrictions (other than Permitted Liens); and

c) there is no action, suit, or proceeding pending or threatened against the Seller that would reasonably be expected to materially impair the value of the Assets.

3.2 Representations and Warranties of Purchaser.

The Purchaser represents and warrants to the Seller that:

(a) it has the right, power, and authority to enter into this Letter of Intent and to acquire the Assets from the Seller;

(b) it is capable of performing its obligations under this Letter of Intent; and

c) there is no action, suit, or proceeding pending or threatened against the Purchaser that would reasonably be expected to materially impair the value of the Assets.

ARTICLE IV: CLOSING

4.1 Closing. The closing of the transactions contemplated hereby shall take place on the date that is 30 days following the date hereof (the "Closing Date").

4.2 Documents Delivered at Closing.

At Closing, the Seller shall deliver to the Purchaser:

(a) a bill of sale conveying good title to the Assets;

(b) an assignment of all right, title and interest in and to the Intellectual Property; and

c) such other documents as may be reasonably requested by the Purchaser.

ARTICLE V: TERMINATION

5.1 Termination. This Letter of Intent shall terminate upon the occurrence of any of the following events:

(a) the failure of either party to perform its obligations under this Letter of Intent; or

c) the occurrence of a material adverse change in the business, operations, assets, or financial condition of the Seller or Purchaser.

ARTICLE VI: GOVERNING LAW

6.1 Governing Law. This Letter of Intent shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Letter of Intent as of the date first above written.

1. LEASE TERM.

The term of this Agreement shall commence on [DATE] (the "Commencement Date") and continue for a period of three (3) years from said date. Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement may be extended for additional periods of one (1) year each upon written notice by either party to the other at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term.

2. RENT AND SECURITY DEPOSIT.

The TENANT shall pay rent in the amount of $5,000 per month, payable on or before the first day of each calendar month during the term hereof. The TENANT shall use the PREMISES only for the purpose of operating a retail business and shall comply with all applicable laws and regulations pertaining thereto.

The TENANT shall maintain liability insurance with limits of at least $1 million per occurrence and property damage insurance with limits of at least $500,000. The LANDLORD shall also maintain liability insurance with limits of at least $1 million per occurrence and property damage insurance with limits of at least $500,000.

6. DEFAULT AND REMEDY.

If the TENANT fails to pay rent when due or otherwise breaches any term or condition of this Agreement, the LANDLORD may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the TENANT. 7. SECURITY DEPOSIT REFUND.

Upon termination of this Agreement, the LANDLORD shall refund the security deposit to the TENANT in accordance with the provisions of Section 6 herein.

8. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

This Merger and Acquisition Letter of Intent (the "Letter")(this "Agreement") is entered into as of this   day of [DATE] (the "Effective Date") by and between:

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1.   Acquisition of Subsidiary. Upon the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in Article III below,

Parent shall acquire all of the Shares from Subsidiary (the "Acquisition"). The purchase price for the Acquisition shall be $10,000,000 (the "Purchase Price").

2.   Representations and Warranties.

Subsidiary represents and warrants to Parent that as of the Effective Date:

a) Subsidiary has all necessary corporate power and authority to enter into this Letter and to sell the Shares;

b) the execution, delivery and performance of this Letter by Subsidiary does not and will not (i) conflict with any agreement, instrument or understanding, oral or written, to which Subsidiary is a party or by which it may be bound; or (ii) violate any material law, rule or regulation applicable to Subsidiary;

c) the Shares are free of any liens or encumbrances, except as disclosed in writing to Parent prior to the Effective Date.

3.   Conditions to Closing.

The closing (the "Closing") of the Acquisition shall take place on the date that is five (5) business days following the date on which all conditions set forth in this Article III have been satisfied or waived (in writing). The conditions to Closing are as follows:

a) Parent and Subsidiary shall have executed and delivered to each other a definitive agreement relating to the Acquisition (the "Definitive Agreement");

b) the Definitif/<|reserved_special_token_149|>ic Agreement shall have been approved by the board of directors of both Parent and Subsidiary;

c) all necessary corporate action required by law or the certificate of incorporation or bylaws of each party to consummate the Acquisition shall have been taken.

4.   Covenants.

Subsidiary covenants that from the Effective Date until the Closing, it will not sell any Shares and will use its best efforts to facilitate the Closing.

5.   Termination.

This Letter may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other if:

a) the Acquisition is not consummated within six (6) months of the Effective Date;
or

b) Parent or Subsidiary gives written notice to the other that it intends to terminate this Letter.

6.   GOVERNING LAW.

This Letter shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

7.   DISPUTE RESOLUTION.

Any dispute arising out of or related to this Letter shall be resolved through arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

1. EMPLOYMENT TERM. Employer shall employ Executive as its Chief Executive Officer for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date. 2. COMPENSATION. Employer shall pay Executive an annual salary of $250,000, payable in accordance with Employer’s regular payroll practices. In addition to such base salary, Executive shall be eligible for a bonus equal to twenty-five percent (25%) of his base salary provided that Employer achieves certain financial targets.

3. BENEFITS. Executive shall be entitled to participate in all benefit plans and programs made available to other executive officers of Employer, including but not limited to health insurance, life insurance, disability insurance, and retirement plan. 6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY. All inventions, improvements, and ideas conceived by Executive during his employment with Employer shall be the sole property of Employer. Executive hereby assigns to Employer all right, title, and interest in and to such inventions, improvements, and ideas.

7. TERMINATION. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other in accordance with Section 1 above. In the event of termination, Executive shall return all property belonging to Employer, including but not limited to documents, files, and equipment.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

9. GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings.

[COMPANY A], a Delaware corporation with principal place of business at 123 Main Street, New York, NY 10001 (the "Buyer"),
and [COMPANY B], a California corporation with principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Los Angeles, CA 90012 (the "Target")

(collectively referred to as the "Parties").

WHEREAS, the Target is desirous of selling its assets and business operations, and the Buyer is desirous of acquiring such assets and operations;
and

WHEREAS, the Parties have determined that it is desirable to enter into this Letter of Intent in order to outline the general terms and conditions under which the proposed transaction will be consummated;
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: PURCHASE AND SALE OF ASSETS

1. The Target shall sell to the Buyer all of its assets, including but not limited to tangible and intangible property, real estate, and goodwill (the "Assets").
The Buyer shall pay for the Assets by paying the sum of $10,000,000 in cash at closing.
2. The Parties agree that the purchase price for the Assets shall be adjusted as necessary to reflect any changes in the Target's financial condition between the date hereof and the closing date.

ARTICLE II: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES

1. The Target represents and warrants to the Buyer that it has good and marketable title to all of the Assets, free from any liens or encumbrances.
The Target further represents and warrants that it has not transferred, assigned, or otherwise disposed of any of the Assets since the date hereof.
2. The Buyer represents and warrants to the Target that it has sufficient funds available to consummate the transaction and shall pay all fees and expenses associated with the closing.

ARTICLE III: CLOSING

1. The Parties agree that the closing shall take place on or before June 15, 2023 (the "Closing Date").
The Closing Date may be extended by written agreement of the Parties.
2. At the Closing, the Buyer shall pay to the Target the purchase price for the Assets and the Target shall deliver to the Buyer all of the Assets, including but not limited to all documents and records relating thereto.

ARTICLE IV: TERMINATION

1. This Letter of Intent may be terminated by either Party upon written notice to the other Party in the event that the proposed transaction is not consummated on or before the Closing Date.
The Parties agree that they shall use their best efforts to consummate the transaction and that any failure to do so shall constitute a material breach of this Letter of Intent.
2. Upon termination, all rights and obligations of the Parties under this Letter of Intent shall cease and terminate, except for any liability for damages or losses arising out of a breach of this Letter of Intent.
