THIS EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT (the "Contract") is entered into on this 25th day of FEBRUARY, TWO THOUSAND AND TWENTY-TWO (the "Effective Date") by and between XYZ CORPORATION, a Delaware corporation ("Company"); and JOHN DOE, an individual ("Executive").

WHEREAS, Company desires to employ Executive as its Chief Executive Officer; and

WHEREAS, Executive is willing to accept such employment on the terms and conditions set forth in this Contract.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. EMPLOYMENT TERM.

a. The term of Executive's employment hereunder (the "Employment Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of five (5) years from such date, unless terminated earlier in accordance with Section 4 below.

b. Upon expiration or termination of the Employment Term, Executive shall be entitled to receive severance pay in an amount equal to two (2) times his annual base salary, which is currently $250,000 per annum.

c. During the Employment Term, Company shall provide Executive with a monthly car allowance of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000).

2. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS.

a. Executive's annual base salary shall be Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($250,000) per year, payable in accordance with Company's regular payroll practices.

b. Executive shall also receive a bonus of Twenty-Five Percent (25%) of his annual base salary, which is currently $62,500 per annum.

c. In addition to the foregoing, Company shall provide Executive with comprehensive health and dental insurance coverage and participation in the Company's 401(k) plan.

3. LIABILITIES AND INDEMNIFICATION.

a. Executive agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Company, its officers, directors, employees and agents (the "Indemnitees") from any claims, damages or expenses arising out of or related to the Executive's employment hereunder, including without limitation any claims arising out of a breach by Executive of this Contract.

b. In the event that Executive is required to pay any such claim or expense, Company shall reimburse Executive for all reasonable legal fees and costs incurred in defending against such claim or expense.

4. TERMINATION.

a. The Employment Term may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other at least thirty (30) days prior to the desired termination date.

b. Upon termination of the Employment Term, Executive shall immediately cease to perform services for Company and shall return all property belonging to Company, including without limitation all confidential information and proprietary materials.

c. In the event that this Contract is terminated due to a material breach by either party, the non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

This Letter of Intent (the "LOI") is entered into on this 23rd day of January, 2023, by and between:
SUNRISE HOLDINGS INC.
(a Delaware corporation) with its principal place of business at 123 Main St, Anytown, USA 12345;
and
GREENFIELD ENTERPRISES LLC
(an Illinois limited liability company) with its principal place of business at 456 Elm St, Othertown, IL 67890.
The Parties (as defined below) intend to enter into a definitive merger agreement (the "Agreement") for the acquisition by Sunrise Holdings Inc. (the "Purchaser") of all outstanding shares of common stock of Greenfield Enterprises LLC (the "Target"), subject to and contingent upon certain conditions set forth in this LOI.
The terms and conditions of this LOI are as follows:
1. The Purchaser shall acquire all outstanding shares of the Target for consideration of $10,000,000, payable in cash at closing.
2. The Target shall be responsible for all liabilities and obligations arising prior to the Effective Date (as defined below).
3. Upon closing, the Purchaser shall assume all liabilities of the Target and shall indemnify the Target against any claims or demands arising from such assumed liabilities.
4. The Effective Date (the "Effective Date") of this LOI is the date on which the Agreement has been executed by both parties and delivered to each other.
The liability cap for the Purchaser's obligation to purchase all outstanding shares of the Target shall be $15,000,000.
The termination conditions of this LOI are as follows: (i) upon mutual written agreement between the Parties; or (ii) if either Party fails to perform any material obligation under this LOI within thirty (30) days after receipt of a written notice from the other Party specifying such failure.
This LOI may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together constitute one and the same instrument. This LOI and the Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.
The Parties hereby agree that this LOI constitutes a binding contract between them, notwithstanding the absence of consideration.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned have executed this LOI as of the date first above written.

/s/ John Smith
Chief Executive Officer, Sunrise Holdings Inc.

/s/ Jane Doe
Managing Member, Greenfield Enterprises LLC.

CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE

THIS COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made and entered into on this 1st day of January, Two Thousand Twenty-Two (2022), by and between:

LANDLORD: XYZ INVESTMENTS, INC., a Delaware Corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA (the “Landlord”);

and TENANT: ABC ENTERPRISES, LLC, a Limited Liability Company with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, USA (the “Tenant”).

WHEREAS, the Landlord is the owner of the premises commonly known as 789 Oak Street, Anytown, USA (the “Premises”); and

WHEREAS, the Tenant desires to lease the Premises for a commercial purpose; and

WHEREAS, the Landlord is willing to grant such lease on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. TERM AND RENTAL RATE
The term of this Lease shall commence on February 1, 2022, and end on January 31, 2025 (the “Term”). The Tenant shall pay a monthly rental rate of $10,000.00 per month.

2. USE OF PREMISES
The Premises shall be used for commercial purposes only. The Tenant shall not sublease or assign this Lease without the prior written consent of the Landlord.

3. MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS
The Tenant shall maintain and repair the Premises in good condition, reasonable wear and tear excepted. The Tenant shall also be responsible for all utilities and services connected to the Premises.

4. INSURANCE
The Tenant shall obtain and maintain liability insurance with limits of at least $1,000,000.00 per occurrence and property damage insurance with limits of at least $500,000.00.

5. DEFAULT AND REMEDIES
If the Tenant fails to comply with any of the terms or conditions of this Lease, the Landlord may terminate this Lease upon written notice to the Tenant. Upon termination, the Tenant shall immediately surrender possession of the Premises to the Landlord.

6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

7. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Anytown, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

8. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT
THIS EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT (the "Agreement") is made and entered into on this day [DATE] by and between:

[COMPANY NAME], a Delaware corporation, with its principal place of business at [ADDRESS] (hereinafter referred to as the "Company"); and

[EXECUTIVE NAME], an individual residing at [ADDRESS] (hereinafter referred to as the "Executive").

WHEREAS, the Company is desirous of employing the Executive as its Chief Operating Officer; and

WHEREAS, the Executive has accepted such employment; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: TERM AND TERMINATION
1. The term of this Agreement shall commence on [START DATE] and continue for a period of three (3) years from the date hereof, subject to earlier termination upon notice in accordance with Section 2 below.
2. Upon receipt of written notice by either party to the other at least thirty (30) days prior thereto, this Agreement may be terminated by either party. The Executive shall not be entitled to any severance pay or benefits upon termination of his employment hereunder, except as provided in ARTICLE VII below.

ARTICLE II: DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
1. The Executive shall devote his full business time and attention to the performance of his duties as Chief Operating Officer for the Company. He shall have such authority and responsibility as may be delegated to him by the Board of Directors, subject to the approval of the President.
2. The Executive shall perform all duties and responsibilities assigned to him in accordance with ARTICLE II above. In addition, he shall:
a) Develop and implement business strategies and plans for the Company;
b) Oversee and direct the operations of the Company;
c) Supervise and manage employees of the Company;
d) Perform such other duties as may be reasonably requested by the President or Board of Directors.

ARTICLE III: COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS
1. The Executive shall receive a salary of $200,000 per annum, payable in equal monthly installments. This amount is subject to annual review and adjustment by the Board of Directors.
2. In addition to his salary, the Executive shall be entitled to participate in the Company's stock option plan (the "Plan") on the same terms as other executives of the Company. The number of shares which he may purchase under the Plan shall be determined by the Committee administering the Plan.
3. The Executive shall also receive a bonus equal to twenty percent (20%) of his annual salary, payable in accordance with ARTICLE III above.

ARTICLE IV: CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
1. The Executive acknowledges that he has access to confidential and proprietary information of the Company during the course of his employment hereunder. He agrees not to disclose such information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Company, except as required by law or regulation.
2. Upon termination of this Agreement, the Executive shall promptly return all documents and materials of the Company in his possession.

ARTICLE V: INDEMNIFICATION
1. The Company agrees to indemnify the Executive against any liability arising out of his actions taken within the scope of his employment hereunder, except as provided below.
2. The Executive shall not be entitled to indemnification for any claims or damages arising out of his gross negligence or willful misconduct.

ARTICLE VI: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION
1. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.
2. Any disputes arising out of this Agreement shall be resolved through arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

ARTICLE VII: SEVERANCE PAY AND BENEFITS
1. Upon termination of his employment hereunder, the Executive shall be entitled to receive a severance payment equal to six (6) months' salary, less any applicable withholdings. The Company may reduce or eliminate this payment in its sole discretion.
2. In addition to the severance payment above, the Executive shall also receive continuation of certain benefits, including health insurance and stock options, for a period of twelve (12) months following termination.

ARTICLE VIII: ENTIRE AGREEMENT
1. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto and supersedes all prior agreements or understandings.
2. No waiver or modification of this Agreement shall be binding unless in writing signed by both parties.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

WHEREAS, the Seller is willing to sell the Shares to the Buyer on the terms and conditions set forth in this LOI;

(b) The Buyer shall pay the Purchase Price in cash upon closing of the transaction;
(c) The Seller shall deliver to the Buyer good and marketable title to the Shares free from any liens or encumbrances.

2. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES.

b) The occurrence of any event that renders the Acquisition impossible or impracticable;

c) The failure of either party to obtain any necessary regulatory approvals.

CONTRACT FOR VENDOR SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT THIS CONTRACT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this    day of March, 2023 by and between XYZ Corporation (the "Vendor") with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA and ABC Inc. (the "Customer") with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, USA (collectively the "Parties").

WHEREAS, the Vendor has agreed to provide certain services to the Customer as more fully described in Exhibit A attached hereto; and

WHEREAS, the Customer has agreed to pay the Vendor for such services in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. DEFINITIONS.

The following definitions shall apply throughout this Agreement: "Service Level" means a measurement of the Vendor's performance in providing the services specified in Exhibit A; and "Service Level Target" means the minimum level of service that the Vendor agrees to provide during any given period of time, as measured by the Service Level. The parties agree that the Service Level shall be calculated based on the following formula: (Total number of incidents resolved) / (Total number of incidents reported). The Service Level Target shall be 95% or higher.

2. SERVICE LEVEL OBLIGATIONS.

The Vendor agrees to provide services in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein, including but not limited to the Service Level Target specified above. The Customer acknowledges that it is responsible for providing all necessary information and cooperation to enable the Vendor to meet its obligations under this Agreement. The parties agree that the Vendor shall be entitled to reasonable compensation for any additional work or services performed in excess of the Service Level Target.

3. PAYMENT TERMS.

The Customer agrees to pay the Vendor for the services provided hereunder in accordance with the payment terms set forth in Exhibit B attached hereto. The parties agree that all payments shall be made in U.S. dollars, by wire transfer or check drawn on a bank located within the United States.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY.

The parties agree to keep confidential all information and materials provided by the other party in connection with this Agreement. This obligation of confidentiality shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination.

5. INDEMNIFICATION.

The Vendor agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless the Customer against any claims, demands or liabilities arising out of or related to the Vendor's performance under this Agreement.

6. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY.

The Vendor's liability for damages shall be limited to the amount of $1,000,000 per occurrence, and in no event shall the total liability exceed $2,000,000 over the term of this Agreement.

7. TERMINATION.

This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party. Upon termination, the Vendor shall return all confidential information and materials provided by the Customer. The parties agree that the termination of this Agreement shall not affect any obligations or liabilities arising prior to the date of termination.

8. GOVERNING LAW.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without giving effect to its conflict of laws principles.

9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION.

Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE

THIS CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 25th day of February, 2022 (the "Effective Date") by and between:

LANDLORD: XYZ Corporation, a New York corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, NY 12345 ("Landlord");

TENANT: ABC Inc., a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, DE 54321 ("Tenant")

WHEREAS, Landlord is the owner of the property commonly known as 789 Park Avenue, Anytown, NY 12345 (the "Premises"); and

WHEREAS, Tenant wishes to lease the Premises for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE 1: TERM AND RENEWAL

1.1 The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of three (3) years from such date.

1.2 Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement may be renewed for additional periods of one (1) year each, provided that written notice of renewal is given by Tenant to Landlord at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term.

ARTICLE 2: RENT AND PAYMENT TERMS

2.1 The annual rent payable by Tenant under this Agreement shall be $250,000 per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of $20,833.33 on or before the first day of each month.

2.2 Tenant shall also pay all taxes, assessments, and other charges levied upon the Premises, as well as all fees and expenses associated with the maintenance and operation of the building.

ARTICLE 3: USE AND OCCUPANCY

3.1 The Premises shall be used solely for office purposes, and Tenant shall not engage in any business or activity that is prohibited by law or that may be deemed a nuisance or disturbance to other tenants in the building.

3.2 Tenant shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances governing the use of the Premises.

ARTICLE 4: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS

4.1 Landlord shall maintain the Premises in good repair and condition, except for damage caused by Tenant's negligence or misuse.

4.2 Tenant shall be responsible for all damages to the Premises caused by its negligence or misuse.

ARTICLE 5: DEFAULT AND REMEDIES

5.1 If Tenant fails to pay rent or other charges when due, Landlord may terminate this Agreement and re-enter the Premises without notice.

5.2 Upon termination of this Agreement, Tenant shall vacate the Premises immediately and surrender possession thereof to Landlord.

ARTICLE 6: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

6.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

6.2 Any disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

This NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 20th day of February, two thousand and twenty-two (2022), by and between

PARTY A: XYZ Corporation (hereinafter referred to as "XYZ" or the "Company")

with an office at:

123 Main Street, Suite 456

Anytown, USA 12345

and PARTY B: John Doe (hereinafter referred to as "John" or the "Recipient")

with an office at:

456 Elm Street, Apt. 789

Othertown, USA 90123

WHEREAS, XYZ is engaged in the development and sale of proprietary software products; and

WHEREAS, John desires to receive certain confidential information regarding XYZ's business operations and proprietary software products.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.

The Company shall disclose to Recipient certain confidential information (the "Confidential Information") relating to its business operations and proprietary software products. Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, all trade secrets, know-how, designs, specifications, formulae, processes, methods of operation, development plans, technical data, documentation, software source code, object code, and any other information that would reasonably be considered confidential or proprietary.

2. OBLIGATIONS OF CONFIDENCE.

Recipient agrees to hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and not disclose it to any third party without prior written authorization from XYZ. Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purposes of evaluating a potential business relationship with XYZ. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its attorneys, accountants, and other professional advisors on a need-to-know basis, provided that such professionals are bound by obligations of confidentiality.

3. TERM AND TERMINATION.

The term of this Agreement shall commence on the date hereof and continue for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, Recipient shall return to XYZ all Confidential Information in its possession, custody, or control, including any copies thereof. Upon written request by XYZ, Recipient shall certify in writing that it has complied with this requirement.

4. LIABILITY AND INDEMNIFICATION.

In no event shall Recipient be liable for any damages arising out of the use of Confidential Information, except to the extent caused by its gross negligence or willful misconduct. In no event shall XYZ be liable for any damages arising out of the use of Confidential Information, except to the extent caused by its gross negligence or willful misconduct.

5. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association, with the seat of arbitration located in Los Angeles, California.

6. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, whether written or oral.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, this Agreement has been executed as of the date first above written.

By:

[Signature]

XYZ Corporation

This Letter of Intent (the "LOI") is entered into on this 22nd day of February, 2023 by and between:

[Company A], a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main St, Anytown, USA ("Buyer");
and [Company B], a California limited liability company with its principal place of business at 456 Elm St, Othertown, CA ("Seller")

WHEREAS, Buyer is desirous of acquiring all the issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of Seller; and
WHEREAS, Seller is willing to sell all such shares to Buyer on the terms and conditions set forth herein;
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. Purchase Price.
The purchase price for the acquisition (the "Purchase Price") shall be $100,000,000 (One Hundred Million Dollars).
2. Closing.
The closing of the transaction (the "Closing") shall take place on or before March 31, 2023 (the "Effective Date"). The Closing shall occur at the offices of Buyer's counsel, and Seller shall deliver to Buyer all necessary documents and instruments required to effectuate the transfer of ownership of Seller.
3. Representations and Warranties.
Seller represents and warrants that:
a) It is the sole owner of all issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of Seller;
b) The business and operations of Seller are conducted in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations;
c) There are no pending or threatened claims against Seller;
d) All representations and warranties contained herein shall survive Closing.
4. Liability Cap.
The liability cap for any breach of the representations and warranties contained herein (the "Liability Cap") shall be $5,000,000 (Five Million Dollars).
5. Termination Conditions.
This LOI may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other if:
a) The transaction does not close on or before June 30, 2023; or
b) Either party fails to perform any of its obligations hereunder in a timely manner.
6. Governing Law.
This LOI shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware. Any disputes arising out of or related to this LOI shall be resolved through arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this LOI as of the date first above written.

CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE

THIS CONTRACT FOR COMMERCIAL LEASE (the "Agreement") is made and entered into on this 12th day of May, Two Thousand Twenty-One (2021), by and between:

LANDLORD: XYZ LANDLORDS LLC, a Delaware limited liability company with its principal place of business at 123 Main St, Anytown, USA 12345 (

"Landlord")

and

TENANT: ABC COMPANY INC., a California corporation with its principal place of business at 456 Elm St, Othertown, CA 90210 (

"Tenant")

FOR and in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

ARTICLE I: TERM AND RENT

The term of this Agreement shall commence on June 1, 2021 (the "Commencement Date") and continue for a period of three (3) years from such date (the "Initial Term"), unless sooner terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof. The rent for the Premises during the Initial Term shall be $10,000.00 per month, payable on or before the first day of each calendar month.

ARTICLE II: USE OF PREMISES

The Tenant shall use and occupy the Premises only for the permitted uses set forth in Schedule A attached hereto. The Tenant shall comply with all applicable laws, ordinances and regulations relating to the use of the Premises,

ARTICLE III: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR

Landlord shall maintain the structural integrity of the building and provide basic maintenance services including but not limited to electrical, plumbing and HVAC systems. Tenant is responsible for maintaining the interior of the Premises in good condition and making necessary repairs to avoid damage to the premises.

ARTICLE IV: INSURANCE

Tenant shall procure and maintain during the term of this Agreement insurance against loss or damage to the Premises, including but not limited to liability insurance and property damage insurance. Landlord shall maintain insurance for any damage caused by Landlord's negligence or failure to maintain the premises.

ARTICLE V: DEFAULT AND REMEDIES

If Tenant fails to pay rent when due, or if Tenant breaches any other term or condition of this Agreement, then upon written notice to Tenant, Landlord may terminate this Agreement and re-enter the Premises. In such event, Tenant shall vacate the Premises and surrender possession thereof to Landlord. Upon termination of this Agreement, Tenant's liability for rent and damages shall continue until a new tenant is found.

ARTICLE VI: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

LANDLORD:

/s/ John Doe

TENANT:

/s/ Jane Smith

If Tenant fails to pay rent when due, or if Tenant breaches any other term or condition of this Agreement, then upon written notice to Tenant, Landlord may terminate this Agreement and re-enter the Premises.

2. The Lease Term may be extended by written agreement between the parties for additional terms of one (1) year each.

ARTICLE II: RENT AND SECURITY DEPOSIT

1. The Tenant shall pay to the Landlord as rent for the Premises, One Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($150,000.00) per annum, payable in equal monthly installments of Twelve Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($12,500.00).

2. The Tenant shall also deposit with the Landlord a Security Deposit in the amount of Twenty-Five Thousand Dollars ($25,000.00), which shall be refundable at the end of the Lease Term, subject to the terms and conditions herein.

ARTICLE III: USE AND OCCUPANCY

2. The Landlord shall also maintain insurance covering the Premises against loss or damage by fire, windstorm, and other hazards, with a minimum coverage limit of One Million Dollars ($1,000,000.00).

1. If the Tenant fails to pay any rent when due, the Landlord may terminate this Agreement and re-enter the Premises.

2. Any dispute arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration, in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

THIS COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this
day of [MONTH], [YEAR] (the "Effective Date") by and between:

LANDLORD: XYZ CORPORATION, a New York corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, Anytown, NY 12345 (hereinafter referred to as the "Landlord"),

and

TENANT: ABC INCORPORATED, an Illinois corporation with its principal place of business at 456 Elm Street, Othertown, IL 67890 (hereinafter referred to as the "Tenant")

ARTICLE I: TERM AND RENT

1. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of three (3) years from such date.

2. During the term of this Agreement, the Tenant shall pay to the Landlord as rent the sum of $10,000 per month, payable in advance on or before the first day of each calendar month.

ARTICLE II: USE OF PREMISES

1. The Tenant agrees to use the leased premises only for commercial purposes and not for any personal or residential use.

2. The Tenant shall comply with all applicable laws, ordinances, and regulations relating to the use of the leased premises.

ARTICLE III: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS

1. The Tenant agrees to maintain the leased premises in a clean and safe condition at all times.

2. The Tenant shall be responsible for all repairs and replacements necessary to keep the leased premises in good condition, except as provided in Article VII.

ARTICLE IV: INSURANCE

1. The Landlord shall procure and maintain during the term of this Agreement a policy of liability insurance covering the leased premises.

2. The Tenant shall procure and maintain during the term of this Agreement a policy of liability insurance covering its activities on the leased premises.

ARTICLE V: DEFAULT AND REMEDIES

1. If the Tenant fails to pay rent when due, or if the Tenant breaches any other provision of this Agreement, the Landlord may terminate this Agreement and re-enter the leased premises without notice.

2. The Tenant shall be liable for all damages suffered by the Landlord as a result of the Tenant's default.

ARTICLE VI: TERMINATION

1. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other, if the other party defaults in any material provision of this Agreement and such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after receipt of notice thereof.

2. Upon termination of this Agreement, the Tenant shall surrender possession of the leased premises to the Landlord.

ARTICLE VII: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

1. The maximum liability of the Landlord for any damage or injury arising from the use of the leased premises shall be $100,000.

2. The Tenant agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Landlord against all claims and demands for damages arising from the use of the leased premises.

ARTICLE VIII: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

1. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

2. Any disputes arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

LANDLORD:

[Signature]

TENANT:

[Signature]

EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT

THIS EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT (the "Agreement") is entered into on this 1st day of March, Two Thousand and Twenty-One (2021) by and between:

[COMPANY NAME], a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at [ADDRESS] ("Employer"); and

[EXECUTIVE NAME], a resident of [STATE] ("Executive").

WHEREAS, Employer desires to employ Executive as its Chief Operating Officer; and

WHEREAS, Executive is willing to accept such employment on the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. TERM AND TERMINATION.

a. The term of this Agreement shall commence on March 15, 2021 (the "Effective Date") and continue for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date (the "Term").

b. Upon expiration of the Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for additional one-year periods unless either party gives written notice of non-renewal to the other at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then current Term.

2. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS.

a. Executive shall receive an annual base salary of $250,000, payable in accordance with Employer's regular payroll practices.

b. Executive shall also be eligible for participation in Employer's annual bonus plan and other benefits as determined by the Board of Directors from time to time.

3. LIABILITY CAP.

a. In no event shall Executive be liable to Employer or its affiliates for any damages, claims, demands, losses, costs, expenses or liabilities (whether direct or indirect) arising out of or related to this Agreement, except in the case of gross negligence or willful misconduct by Executive.

b. The aggregate liability of Executive to Employer and its affiliates under this Agreement shall not exceed $1 million.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY.

a. Executive acknowledges that all Confidential Information (as defined below) is the exclusive property of Employer and its affiliates.

b. Executive agrees to keep confidential and not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without Employer's prior written consent.

c. Upon termination of this Agreement, Executive shall promptly return to Employer all documents and materials containing Confidential Information in Executive's possession, custody or control.

5. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.

a. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

b. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

6. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.

a. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings.

b. This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties.

The Executive’s employment with the Company shall commence on February 15th, two thousand twenty-one (2021) and continue for a period of three (3) years from such date (the “Initial Term”). Upon expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement may be extended for additional one-year periods, subject to the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth. The Executive shall receive a base salary of One Hundred Thousand Dollars ($100,000) per annum, payable in accordance with the Company’s regular payroll practices. In addition to the base salary, the Executive shall be eligible to participate in the Company’s annual bonus plan (the “Bonus Plan”), which provides for bonuses based on the Company’s performance.

3. BENEFITS.

The Executive shall be entitled to receive certain benefits during his employment with the Company, including but not limited to:

a. Health insurance;

b. Life insurance;

c. Disability insurance;

d. Retirement plan matching contributions;

e. Vacation time;

f. Holidays.

4. CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE.

The Executive shall not disclose any confidential information of the Company to any third party, and shall not use such information for his own benefit or the benefit of any other person or entity.

5. GOVERNING LAW.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York.

6. DISPUTE RESOLUTION.

Any disputes arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

All notices required or permitted to be given under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered personally, by certified mail, or by facsimile transmission.

8. AMENDMENTS.

This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in a writing signed by both parties hereto.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings, and agreements between the parties hereto.

10. GOVERNING PRINCIPLES.

The parties hereto acknowledge that this Agreement was negotiated at arm’s length and in good faith,

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

ARTICLE 1: DEFINITIONS

1.1. For purposes of this Agreement, 'Confidential Information' shall mean any and all information disclosed by one party to the other, whether orally or in writing, that is marked as confidential or would reasonably be considered confidential.

1.2. The terms 'Company Data' and 'Customer Data' are defined in Schedule 1 attached hereto.

ARTICLE 2: SOFTWARE USE AND PROVISION

2.1. The Company shall provide the Customer with access to the Software via a web-based interface, as may be updated from time to time by the Company (the 'Interface').

2.2. The Customer agrees to use the Interface only for its own internal business purposes and not for any commercial or resale purpose.

ARTICLE 3: CUSTOMER RESPONSIBILITIES

3.1. The Customer shall be responsible for ensuring that all users of the Software comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

3.2. The Customer shall provide the Company with accurate and complete information as required by the Interface, including but not limited to, name, address, email address, and other contact details.

ARTICLE 4: FEES AND PAYMENT TERMS

4.1. The Customer shall pay the Company the fees set forth in Schedule 2 attached hereto (the 'Fees') for access to the Software.

4.2. The Fees are non-refundable and non-cancelable except as otherwise provided in this Agreement or by law.

ARTICLE 5: PROPRIETARY RIGHTS

5.1. The Company retains all right, title, and interest in and to the Software and the Interface, including but not limited to all patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights thereto.

5.2. The Customer acknowledges that it has no right, title, or interest in or to the Software or the Interface, except as expressly granted in this Agreement.

ARTICLE 6: CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

6.1. Each party agrees to keep confidential all Confidential Information disclosed by the other party and shall not disclose such information to any third party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.

6.2. The Customer acknowledges that the Software and the Interface contain proprietary information and trade secrets of the Company, and agrees not to reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the Software or the Interface.

ARTICLE 7: INDEMNIFICATION AND LIABILITY

7.1. The Company shall indemnify and hold harmless the Customer against any third-party claims arising out of the use of the Software by the Customer in accordance with this Agreement.

7.2. In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, special, incidental, or consequential damages, including but not limited to loss of business profits, business interruption, or loss of data, arising out of the use of the Software or failure to perform any obligation under this Agreement.

ARTICLE 8: TERM AND TERMINATION

8.1. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue until terminated in accordance with this Article 8.

8.2. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other party in the event of a material breach by the other party, which remains uncured for a period of thirty (30) days from the date of written notice.

ARTICLE 9: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

9.1. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware.

9.2. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

(c) "SaaS Products" means the software as a service products licensed to Customer under this Agreement.

3. TERM AND TERMINATION

(b) Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, Customer shall immediately cease using the SaaS Products.

10. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings.

ARTICLE I: DEFINITIONS

1. For purposes hereof, "Transaction" means the acquisition by Investor of all Shares from Company.

2. The term "Company Materials" means any and all documents, records, reports, files, correspondence, memoranda, and other materials (in whatever form or medium) relating to the business, operations, properties, assets, liabilities, financial condition, or prospects of the Company.

3. The term "Investor Materials" means any and all documents, records, reports, files, correspondence, memoranda, and other materials (in whatever form or medium) relating to Investor's business, operations, properties, assets, liabilities, financial condition, or prospects.

ARTICLE II: TRANSACTION TERMS AND CONDITIONS

1. The Company shall use its reasonable best efforts to consummate the Transaction on or before June 30, 2022 (the "CLOSING DATE").

2. Investor agrees to purchase all Shares from the Company for a consideration of $10,000,000.

3. The Company shall provide Investor with access to its premises and facilities for the purpose of inspecting and reviewing the Company Materials.

4. The parties agree that the Transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including but not limited to the receipt by Investor of a favorable opinion from its counsel as to the matters set forth in Annex A hereto.

ARTICLE III: CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE

1. Each party agrees to keep confidential all information and materials relating to the other party (the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION").

ARTICLE IV: REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES

1. Company represents and warrants that:

a. it has good title to all Shares free from liens, encumbrances, or other claims;

c. the Shares are not subject to any voting trust or similar agreement.

2. Investor represents and warrants that:

a. it has good title to all shares of capital stock of Investor free from liens, encumbrances, or other claims;

b. it has the right, power, and authority to enter into this LOI; and

c. it is financially capable of consummating the Transaction.

ARTICLE V: TERMINATION

1. This LOI may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other party in accordance with Annex B hereto.

2. Upon termination, all Confidential Information shall remain confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this LOI as of the date first above written.

dental insurance.

The Executive shall not disclose any such confidential information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Company.

ARTICLE 5: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.

This Merger Agreement and Plan of Merger (the "Agreement") is entered into as of this 23rd day of March, 2023, by and between

SILVER OAK HOLDINGS INC. ("Silver Oak"), a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main Street, New York, NY 10001,

WHEREAS, the Boards of Directors of Silver Oak and Green Tech have approved this Agreement; and

WHEREAS, Silver Oak and Green Tech desire to enter into this Agreement to effectuate a merger (the "Merger") between Silver Oak and Green Tech under Section 251C of the Delaware General Corporation Law;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. DEFINITIONS.

For purposes of this Agreement, capitalized terms used but not defined shall have the meanings assigned to them in that certain Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation of Silver Oak.

2. THE MERGER.

The Merger shall be consummated by filing articles of merger (the "Articles of Merger") with the Secretary of State of Delaware, which Articles of Merger shall state that Green Tech is surviving corporation in the Merger and that all shares of common stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the Effective Time (as defined below) shall be converted into the right to receive one share of common stock of Silver Oak for each share of common stock so issuable.

3. EFFECTIVE TIME.

The Merger shall become effective at such time as the Articles of Merger are filed with the Secretary of State of Delaware (the "Effective Time") or at such other time as the parties may agree in writing.

4. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES.

Silver Oak and Green Tech each represent and warrant to the other that:

(a) they have all necessary authority, power and right to enter into this Agreement and to consummate the transactions contemplated hereunder;

(b) the execution and delivery of this Agreement by them and the performance of their respective obligations hereunder do not and will not violate any applicable law or regulation;

(c) there is no action pending or threatened against either of them that would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on its business, properties, assets, liabilities or financial condition;

d. each of them has all necessary authority, power and right to make the representations and warranties contained in this Agreement.

5. COVENANTS.

Silver Oak and Green Tech covenant and agree that:

(a) they shall use their respective best efforts to effectuate the Merger as soon as practicable after the Effective Time;

(b) Silver Oak shall, at its expense, prepare the Articles of Merger and file them with the Secretary of State of Delaware; and

c. Green Tech shall, at its expense, deliver to Silver Oak a certified copy of the Articles of Merger.

6. DEFAULTS AND REMEDIES.

If either party fails to perform or observe any covenant or agreement contained in this Agreement, the other party may pursue all remedies available to it under applicable law.

7. TERMINATION.

This Agreement may be terminated prior to the Effective Time by mutual written consent of the parties hereto;

8. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

9. ENTIRE AGREEMENT.

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement among the parties hereto relating to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings, whether written or oral.

10. AMENDMENTS AND WAIVERS.

This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by the parties hereto; provided that no failure or delay in exercising any right or remedy shall operate as a waiver thereof,
or

11. NOTICES.

All notices, requests and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered personally or sent by facsimile transmission, email or certified mail, return receipt requested.

12. DEFINITIONS AND CONSTRUCTION.

In the interpretation and construction of this Agreement, the following rules of construction shall apply:

(a) headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of any provision;

(b) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; and

c. words importing gender also import the other genders.

13. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law,

This Letter of Intent (the "LOI") is entered into on this 23rd day of February, Two Thousand Twenty-One by and between XYZ Corporation (the "Seller") and ABC Holdings Inc. (the "Buyer"), collectively referred to as the "Parties.

WHEREAS, the Seller is the owner of one hundred percent (100%) of the issued and outstanding shares of XYZ Subsidiary Corp., a Delaware corporation (the "Target"); and

WHEREAS, the Buyer desires to acquire the Target from the Seller; and

WHEREAS, the Parties wish to enter into this LOI in order to outline the terms and conditions of the proposed transaction.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the Parties agree as follows:

1. The Purchase Price shall be One Hundred Million Dollars ($100,000,000) payable at closing (the "Closing Date").
The Closing Date shall be within one hundred and eighty (180) days from the date hereof.

2. The Seller shall deliver to the Buyer a copy of the Target's financial statements for the two (2) years immediately preceding the date hereof, which shall have been audited by a reputable accounting firm.
The Buyer reserves the right to request additional information and due diligence as it deems necessary in connection with its review of the proposed transaction.

3. The Parties agree that the Target shall not engage in any material transactions (including but not limited to mergers, acquisitions or asset sales) without the prior written consent of the Buyer.

4. This LOI may be terminated by either Party upon written notice to the other Party in the event that the proposed transaction is not consummated within one hundred and eighty (180) days from the date hereof.

5. The Parties agree to use their reasonable best efforts to negotiate and execute a definitive agreement relating to the proposed transaction on or before the Closing Date.

6. The Seller shall bear all costs and expenses in connection with this LOI, including but not limited to due diligence and other fees associated therewith.
The Buyer shall bear its own costs and expenses associated with its review of the proposed transaction.

7. This LOI constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties relating to the proposed transaction and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings and agreements between the Parties.

8. The Parties acknowledge that this LOI is intended to be a non-binding document and does not create any contractual obligations between the Parties.

9. This LOI may be amended or modified only in writing signed by both Parties.

10. The laws of the State of Delaware shall govern the validity, interpretation and enforcement of this LOI,
and the courts of the State of Delaware shall have jurisdiction over all disputes arising out of or related to this LOI.

PACIFIC CORP. a Delaware corporation (the "Parent"); and 

SUBSIDIARY INC. a Delaware corporation (the "Subsidiary")

WHEREAS, the parties wish to consummate a merger of the Subsidiary with and into the Parent in accordance with this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

 ARTICLE I DEFINITIONS

1.1 "Affiliate" means any Person that directly or indirectly Controls, is Controlled by, or is under common Control with a Party.

1.2 "Business Day" means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or federal holiday in the United States.

1.3 "Control" (including the correlative meanings of "Controls," "controlled by" and "under common control with") as used herein shall have the meaning given to such term under Section 424(e) of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended.

ARTICLE II MERGER

2.1 The Parent and the Subsidiary agree to consummate a merger (the "Merger") in accordance with this Agreement, whereby the Subsidiary shall be merged with and into the Parent (the "Parent Survives"), and the separate corporate existence of the Subsidiary shall cease.

ARTICLE III EFFECTIVE TIME AND CLOSING

3.1 The Merger shall be effective as of the Effective Time (as defined in Section 2.4).

3.2 The closing of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement (the "Closing") shall occur at the offices of Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, New York, New York.

ARTICLE IV LIABILITIES AND OBLIGATIONS

4.1 All Liabilities and Obligations of the Subsidiary shall remain with the Subsidiary until the Effective Date, after which they will be assumed by the Parent in accordance with this Agreement.

ARTICLE V REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES

(a) it has full power and authority to execute, deliver and perform this Agreement;

(c) this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding obligation of each Party.

ARTICLE VI COVENANTS AND OBLIGATIONS

6.1 Each of the parties agrees to:

(a) use its commercially reasonable efforts to consummate the Merger on or before the Closing Date;

(b) obtain all necessary regulatory approvals; and

(c) comply with all applicable laws.

ARTICLE VII TERMINATION

7.1 This Agreement may be terminated by either party if the other party fails to perform any of its obligations hereunder, or if the Merger is not consummated on or before the Closing Date.

7.2 In the event of termination, all rights and obligations under this Agreement shall cease.

ARTICLE VIII GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware.

8.2 Any disputes arising from or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

CONTRACT FOR EXECUTIVE EMPLOYMENT

This Execu	ive Employment Contract (the "Agreement") is made and entered into on this 

day of March, 2023 (the "Effective Date"), by and between

[COMPANY NAME], a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at [ADDRESS] (the "Company"); and

[EXECUTIVE NAME], an individual residing at [ADDRESS] (the "Executive")

WHEREAS, the Company desires to employ the Executive as its Chief Operating Officer; and

WHEREAS, the Executive is willing to accept such employment on the terms and conditions set forth herein;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. EMPLOYMENT TERM.
The Company agrees to employ the Executive as its Chief Operating Officer for a period of three (3) years from the Effective Date (the "Employment Term"). The Employment Term may be extended by written agreement between the parties for additional periods of one (1) year each, but in no event shall such extension exceed five (5) years from the Effective Date.

2. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES.
The Executive agrees to perform his duties as Chief Operating Officer in a manner consistent with the Company's policies and procedures, and in accordance with the bylaws of the Company. The Executive shall be responsible for all matters relating to the operation of the Company, including but not limited to:

(a) Overseeing the day-to-day operations of the Company;

(b) Developing and implementing business strategies and plans;

c) Supervising and directing the employees of the Company;

d) Managing the financial affairs of the Company.

3. COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS.
The Company agrees to pay the Executive a base salary of $250,000 per year, plus an annual bonus of up to 20% of his base salary based on the Company's performance. The Executive shall also be entitled to participate in the Company's stock option plan and other employee benefit plans as may be adopted by the Board of Directors from time to time.

4. LIABILITIES AND INDEMNIFICATION.
The Company agrees to indemnify the Executive against any claims, damages, or expenses arising out of his service as Chief Operating Officer, except in cases of willful misconduct or gross negligence on the part of the Executive. The Executive shall also be entitled to receive reimbursement for all reasonable expenses incurred by him in connection with his duties as Chief Operating Officer.

5. TERMINATION.
The Employment Term may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other, but only in accordance with the following conditions:

(a) Death or Disability. The Employment Term shall terminate immediately upon the death or disability of the Executive;

(b) Willful Misconduct. The Company may terminate the Employment Term if the Executive engages in willful misconduct or gross negligence that results in material harm to the Company.

(c) Breach of Agreement. Either party may terminate the Employment Term if the other party breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days after written notice thereof;

d) Change in Control. The Employment Term shall terminate immediately upon a change in control of the Company, as defined in Section 409A of the Internal Revenue Code.

6. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.

7. AMENDMENTS AND WAIVERS.
This Agreement may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by both parties. No waiver by either party of any provision hereof shall be effective unless explicitly stated to be such in writing and signed by the party against whom enforcement is sought.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.

c) notify the receiving party of any changes to the confidential information.

MERGER & ACQUISITION LETTER OF INTENT
This Letter of Intent (the "LOI") is entered into on this 20th day of March, 2023 by and between:

ACME INCORPORATED (the "Buyer")
with a principal place of business at 123 Main St., New York, NY 10001
and

XYZ ENTERPRISES (the "Seller")
with a principal place of business at 456 Elm St., Chicago, IL 60611.

WHEREAS, the Seller is the owner of all right, title and interest in and to the XYZ Business (as defined below); and

WHEREAS, the Buyer desires to acquire from the Seller all of the issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of the XYZ Business;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties hereto agree as follows:

1. Acquisition.
The Buyer shall acquire one hundred percent (100%) of the issued and outstanding shares of capital stock of the XYZ Business for a purchase price of One Billion Dollars ($1,000,000,000). The purchase price shall be paid in cash at closing.

2. Representations and Warranties.
The Seller represents and warrants to the Buyer that:

a) The Seller has good title to the XYZ Business free from any liens or encumbrances;

b) The financial statements of the XYZ Business have been accurately prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles;

c) The business operations of the XYZ Business are conducted in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.

3. Covenants.
The Seller covenants to:

a) Maintain the present business operations of the XYZ Business until closing;

b) Keep the Buyer informed of any material changes in the financial condition or business operations of the XYZ Business;

c) Cooperate with the Buyer in the acquisition and integration of the XYZ Business.

4. Termination.
This LOI may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other if:

a) The closing has not occurred within six (6) months from the date hereof;

b) Either party fails to fulfill any of its obligations under this LOI.

5. Governing Law.
This LOI shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

6. Entire Agreement.
This LOI, including all exhibits and schedules hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, understandings and agreements between them.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this LOI as of the date first above written.

MERGER & ACQUISITION LETTER OF INTENT
This Letter of Intent (the "Letter") is entered into on this 10th day of February, 2023, by and between:

ACME INCORPORATED ("Acme") a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 123 Main St., New York, NY 10001,
and

ZYX CORPORATION ("Zyx"), a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 456 Elm St., Chicago, IL 60611.

WHEREAS, Acme is engaged in the business of manufacturing and selling widgets; and

WHEREAS, Zyx is engaged in the business of designing and developing software applications;

NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

1. The proposed transaction shall be a merger of Acme into Zyx (the "Merger") subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Letter.
2. Upon consummation of the Merger, all outstanding shares of common stock of Acme will be cancelled and retired.
3. Zyx will assume all liabilities of Acme as if such liabilities were its own, up to a maximum amount of $10,000,000 (the "Liability Cap").

[EMPLOYEE NAME]: JAMES R. SMITH

ARTICLE I: TERM AND TERMINATION

2. Termination. This Contract may be terminated by either party upon written notice to the other in the event that the other party commits any material breach of this Contract, which breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after receipt of such notice. ARTICLE II: COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

1. Salary. The Company shall pay to Employee an annual salary of $250,000, payable in equal monthly installments on the last day of each month. In addition thereto, the Company shall pay Employee a bonus of twenty (20) percent of his base salary annually, paid out in two (2) equal installments on the fifteenth and thirtieth days of June and December.

ARTICLE III: CONFIDENTIALITY AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

1. Confidentiality. The Employee agrees to keep confidential all trade secrets, business methods, and other proprietary information relating to the Company's business, which may come into his possession or knowledge during the term of this Contract.

2. Intellectual Property. All inventions, designs, patents, trademarks, copyrights, and other intellectual property rights developed by the Employee during the term of this Contract shall be the sole and exclusive property of the Company.

ARTICLE IV: INDEMNIFICATION AND HOLD HARMLESS

1. Indemnification. The Company agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Employee against any claims, demands, or causes of action arising out of or related to the performance of his duties hereunder.

ARTICLE V: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

1. Governing Law. This Contract shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any principles of conflicts of law.

2. Jurisdiction. Any disputes arising out of or related to this Contract shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
